name: Multiplying and Dividing Fractions
whatIsIt: To MULTIPLY fractions, multiply the tops and multiply the bottoms: 2/3 × 4/5 = 8/15. The word 'of' means multiply — half OF a quarter is 1/2 × 1/4 = 1/8. To DIVIDE by a fraction, FLIP the second one and multiply (multiply by its reciprocal): 1/2 ÷ 1/4 = 1/2 × 4/1 = 2. Simplify the answer at the end.
whyNeeded: Multiplying and dividing fractions runs through recipes (half of ¾ cup), scaling, rates, probability, and algebra. The flip-and-multiply rule for division is one of the handiest tricks in maths.
realLifeUses: Half of a three-quarter cup of sugar (1/2 × 3/4)
How many quarter-litre glasses fill from 2 litres (2 ÷ 1/4)
Scaling a recipe down by a fraction
Sharing a fraction of a fraction of a cake

vocabulary.meaning: A fraction flipped upside down: the reciprocal of 3/4 is 4/3.

story.title: Half of Grandma's Recipe
story.text: Grandma's laddoo recipe needs 3/4 cup of ghee, but Neel wants to make just half. 'How much ghee?' he asks. Half OF three-quarters means 1/2 × 3/4. Neel multiplies tops and bottoms: 1×3 = 3, 2×4 = 8. 'Three-eighths of a cup!' Grandma checks — spot on.
story.extractedProblem: How much is half of 3/4 cup? (1/2 × 3/4)

visual.caption: Split the whole into 4 columns (quarters) and 2 rows (halves) → 8 equal boxes. 3 shaded of 8 = 3/8.

teachingGallery.title: Multiply across; divide by flipping
teachingGallery.note: Tops × tops, bottoms × bottoms; to divide, flip and multiply.

examples.caption: A third of a third is one ninth.

standardMethod.summary: Multiply: tops × tops over bottoms × bottoms, then simplify. Divide: multiply by the reciprocal of the second fraction (flip and multiply).
steps: 'of' means multiply. A whole number is that number over 1.
Divide: keep the first, FLIP the second, and multiply (× its reciprocal).
Simplify the answer to lowest terms at the end.
= 2. (There are two quarters in a half.)

commonMistakes.mistake: Adding tops and bottoms when multiplying (2/3 × 3/5 → 5/8).
commonMistakes.fix: For MULTIPLY, multiply straight across: tops × tops, bottoms × bottoms — don't add.

commonMistakes.mistake: Dividing fractions straight across instead of flipping the second.
commonMistakes.fix: To divide, keep the first, FLIP the second, then multiply: ÷ 1/4 becomes × 4/1.

teachBackPrompt: Use a recipe: show a grown-up how to find half of 3/4 cup (1/2 × 3/4 = 3/8), and how many 1/4-cup scoops fill 2 cups (2 ÷ 1/4 = 8) — explaining the flip-and-multiply trick for division.

revisionCard.summary: Multiply: tops × tops over bottoms × bottoms, then simplify. 'Of' = multiply. Divide: keep–flip–multiply (× the reciprocal): a ÷ b/c = a × c/b. 1/2 ÷ 1/4 = 1/2 × 4/1 = 2. Never add across when multiplying.
